color_profiles.name: Classic Brassy Pyrite
color_profiles.cause: Metallic luster from d-band electronic structure of iron sulfide; brass-yellow reflectance.

color_profiles.name: Iridescent Tarnished Pyrite
color_profiles.cause: Thin-film iron oxide/hydroxide layer from surface oxidation (FeS2 → Fe oxide + H2SO4).

color_profiles.name: Pyrite Sun / Dollar Pyrite
colors: flat disc with radiating crystal striations
color_profiles.cause: Radial crystal growth in confined space (coal seam cleavage planes).

visual_notes: Pyrite is mineralogy's great impersonator — 'fool's gold' for millennia. The striated faces of pyrite cubes (from oscillation between cube and pyritohedron) are diagnostic. Iron cross twins — two interlocking pyritohedra — are among mineralogy's most architecturally perfect specimens. Pyrite suns (flat radiating discs) are unique and immediately recognizable forms.
